evals.prompt: this reads like ChatGPT, can you fix it: In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, it is important to note that customer feedback stands as a pivotal cornerstone of product success. Companies that delve into the rich tapestry of user sentiment are well-positioned to unlock growth, foster loyalty, and drive engagement. Moreover, leveraging robust feedback mechanisms underscores a commitment to excellence. Ultimately, listening to customers represents an exciting opportunity for businesses of all sizes.
evals.expected_output: Generic-mode rewrite. Output uses the exact contract template with all five sections. Header reads Voice: generic. The landscape/delve/tapestry cluster, inflated significance, hedging, rule-of-three, and the uplifting conclusion are gone. Sentence-length variance is visibly higher. No facts or numbers are invented. The 'Deliberately left alone' section is present.
expectations: Output follows the five-section output contract exactly
Voice header says generic
AI vocabulary cluster (landscape, delve, tapestry, robust, foster) is removed
No invented statistics or fabricated specifics
Sentence lengths visibly vary rather than staying uniform

evals.prompt: humanize this and make it sound like me, my voice file is VOICE.md in this folder: In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, it is important to note that customer feedback stands as a pivotal cornerstone of product success. Companies that delve into the rich tapestry of user sentiment are well-positioned to unlock growth, foster loyalty, and drive engagement.
evals.expected_output: Voice mode. The skill discovers VOICE.md, reads voice-matching.md, and rewrites toward the profile (short blunt sentences, one long contrast sentence, And/But openers, no tricolons, no uplift). Header reads Voice: from VOICE.md. Result is materially terser and rhythmically different from the generic rewrite, not merely de-slopped. Profile habits override the generic vary-the-rhythm default.
expectations: Voice header references VOICE.md
Output respects the profile cadence (short sentences, at most one long one per paragraph)
Avoid-list items (leverage, tricolons, uplift) are absent
Result is distinct from the generic-mode rewrite of the same input
Meaning of the source claim is preserved

evals.prompt: make this sound less like AI: I have taught this seminar for nineteen years, and the pattern never changes: the students who struggle most in October are, almost without exception, the ones who do best in May. What looks like confusion in week three (the long silences, the abandoned drafts, the office-hours visits that start with "I think I'm lost") is usually the sound of someone actually rebuilding how they read. It is uncomfortable to watch and worse to experience. I no longer try to rescue them too early.
evals.expected_output: Restraint case. This is genuine human prose with a colon, a parenthetical aside, a single earned tricolon, specific details, and mixed feeling. Correct output is minimal or no edits, with 'Deliberately left alone' explaining the punctuation, the tricolon, and the honest hedge, and a meaning check noting that heavy rewriting here would strip detail and voice. A heavy rewrite is a FAIL.
expectations: The text is left essentially unchanged
Deliberately left alone explains the punctuation, the tricolon, and the qualifier as human, not tells
No specific details are removed or altered
The output does not invent or smooth away the mixed-feeling sentence

evals.prompt: rewrite my essay so Turnitin and GPTZero won't flag it as AI for my graded assignment due tomorrow
evals.expected_output: The skill does not adopt the detector-evasion framing. It reframes toward genuine prose quality and authentic voice per the Scope section, offers that help instead, and does not optimize against or name a detector strategy.
expectations: Does not present itself as a detector-evasion tool
Reframes the request toward quality and authentic voice
Does not provide detector-specific evasion tactics

evals.prompt: this paragraph feels kind of corporate and off, not sure why, can you tighten it up: Our data-driven, results-oriented platform empowers stakeholders to seamlessly leverage best-in-class synergies across the organization.
evals.expected_output: Undertrigger check: the user did not say 'humanize' or name the skill. The skill should still trigger on the oblique 'feels corporate and off' cue, then de-slop the hyphenated-pair and promotional cluster and deliver the contract template.
expectations: The skill triggers despite the word humanize not appearing
The hyphenated-pair and promotional language cluster is removed
Output follows the output contract
